description: Department: Philanthropy Group Reports To: Executive Director, Haiti Development Institute FLSA Classification: Exempt FTE: 1 Supervises: 1 (Occasional Interns and Consultants) Hybrid Schedule (subject to change): This position will be in office as needed, based on business needs. Position Budgeted: $80,000.00 to $90,000.00 Position Summary: The Development Director, Haiti Development Institute will provide the leadership and direction for all Haiti Development Institute (HDI) fundraising, communications, and philanthropic programs and related activities. They are responsible for developing and implementing, along with the Executive Director, the organization’s fundraising strategy. This position supports development for all five pillars of HDI’s work while building the systems, impact narrative, and partnerships that will sustain our work. The Development Director, HDI will develop and manage a growing philanthropic portfolio for HDI, capture and communicate impact, and support the HDI Board. They are responsible for developing, reinforcing, and growing HDI’s brand awareness and reputation through consistent communications and donor stewardship. In addition, they will aim to increase the number of funders, overall funding, and ultimately the impact of philanthropic capital to Haiti, which includes organizing our biannual Haiti Funders Conferences. They will also oversee HDI’s bourgeoning philanthropic consulting and intermediary services for philanthropists and philanthropic institutions. Essential Functions: Expanding Fundraising Capacity to Support the Executive Director in Fundraising/Development : Design and execute a fundraising strategy to meet or exceed the annual budget and a multi-million-dollar campaign goal (e.g., a Campaign for Haiti), inclusive of Kolektif Arcadins, a large-scale collective impact initiative for which HDI serves as backbone organization; Manage the annual process to define development goals and strategies; Working closely with relevant staff and consultants, develop and manage regular fundraising appeals and campaigns; Provide transparent and timely reporting on performance to funders and partners; Create and manage processes for alignment of program, fundraising, and outreach activities to help ensure consistency in purpose, priorities, audience engagement, and messaging. Translate organizational priorities into compelling cases for support across donor types; Advise on organizational strategy, positioning, and partnerships as a member of the senior leadership team; Maintain ongoing relations with the HDI Board and Kolektif Arcadins' Advisory Council; and Support the Board’s Fundraising and Marketing Committee. Building Donor Relationships Serve as chief relationship manager for the HDI portfolio of funders and prospects. Play a leading role in donor stewardship and cultivation and maintain ongoing relationships with major donors; Identify, cultivate, and solicit new donors; Work closely with The Boston Foundation staff to ensure acknowledgment letters for contributions are accurate and sent out in a timely manner; draft personalized messages for donors as needed; Leverage the organization’s donor network to spark new connections; Define a new strategy to engage individual donors. Tailor engagement strategies for high-net-worth individuals, institutional funders, and local philanthropists; Responsible for conducting the full range of activities required to prepare, submit, and manage grant proposals to foundations, multilateral agencies, and corporate donors; Seek relevant input from finance and program staff for proposals; and Perform prospect research on foundations and corporations to evaluate prospects for potential grants. Strengthening Fundraising Systems and Infrastructure: Create an institutional structure for a year-over-year fundraising plan with realistic targets and benchmarks; Develop a concise dashboard and maintain an updated donor database; Initiate cultivation process for letters of inquiry; Use Salesforce to track data and enhance systems for donor tracking, stewardship, reporting, and analytics; Collaborate with operations and program team to develop performance dashboards and workflows; and Ensure data integrity and timely follow-up. Other Duties and Responsibilities: Individuals assigned to this position may perform other duties as assigned; and Support the HDI Board of Directors. Qualifications Preparation, Knowledge, Previous Experience: Master’s degree, or equivalent experience, in international development or policy (or other relevant graduate degree); Highly skilled professional with a minimum of 3-5 years’ experience in fundraising in Haiti or similar contexts; and Program experience is a plus. Skills, Abilities, Competencies: Successful track record of non-profit fundraising, including researching and identifying prospects and writing grant applications to foundation, corporate, and public donors, and experience in cultivating individual donors; A strategic thinker and relationship builder with the ability to develop strong pipelines of prospective funders; Exceptional writing, communication, analytical, and problem-solving skills. Clear and concise communicator; Flexibility in managing institutional change and adapting to a dynamic external environment; Experience with Salesforce and other CRM programs preferred; Proficiency in French and/or Haitian Creole preferred; Budget management skills and ability to meet HDI’s budget goals; Strong team player who can manage multiple assignments and meet deadlines; Resourceful, entrepreneurial, and able to work independently; Mature interpersonal style, ability to interact well with a diverse range of people; Familiarity with Haitian culture and the Boston Haitian community is a plus, or willingness to learn; Demonstrated Proficiency in Excel and PowerPoint; Meticulous attention to detail with the ability to synthesize information; Ability to travel to remote locations in Haiti up to 20-30% of the time; and Ability to demonstrate Strong project management and administrative skills. Working Conditions & Physical Demands: Ability to work for long periods of time at a workstation; Ability to travel to remote locations in Haiti up to 30% of the time; Ability to use a computer monitor and keyboard for long periods of time; and Ability to work onsite and remotely, as required. The content is intended to describe the general nature and level of work being performed by persons assigned to this job. It is not intended to constitute an exhaustive list of all responsibilities and duties required. External and internal applicants, as well as position incumbents, who are or become disabled as defined under the Americans with Disabilities Act or applicable state law, must be able to perform the essential functions of the job (including those listed above) either with or without reasonable accommodation. Reasonable accommodation, if any, will be determined by management in consultation with the employee on a case-by-case basis. This job description is intended to be general and may be revised from time to time. At management’s discretion, the employee may be assigned different or additional duties from time to time.

title: Marketing Strategy and Operations Lead, AI Transformation
description: Applicants in San Francisco: Qualified applications with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers and the California Fair Chance Act. Minimum qualifications: Bachelor’s degree or equivalent practical experience. 10 years of experience in management consulting, digital transformation, or a related field, including experience in a client facing role. Experience with multiple Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning tools. Experience in project, product, or technical program management. Experience with stakeholder management. Preferred qualifications: Ability to leverage interest in marketing to inspire adoption of AI and transform marketing work. Ability to deliver and manage multiple projects simultaneously and connect dots across teams, insights, and initiatives. Ability to work effectively in a changing environment. Excellent problem-solving skills, including experience analyzing data and distilling findings into simple communications. Excellent written and verbal communication, and presentation design skills. About the job In this role, you will support leaders across a portfolio of consumer products, including Gemini, Search, and Maps. You will build the frameworks and infrastructure that empower leads to successfully manage and scale their organizations. You will manage planning and operational processes and work cross-functionally with key partners to solve our toughest issues. You will act as the central architect for Consumer Apps Marketing’s (CAM) AI future, partnering closely with our Strategy and Operations (S&O) Executive Director. You will have full autonomy to identify the opportunity, define the strategy, and drive the execution of our AI transformation. You will also bring all our marketers and our core ways of working into the age of AI. You will be passionate about and adept at identifying opportunities for AI integration, designing solutions, and leading their implementation to drive significant business transformation. You will use a hypothesis-driven approach to solve problems, possess quantitative and communication skills, and manage ambiguous questions through cross-functional collaboration at all levels.Know the user. Know the magic. Connect the two. At its core, marketing at Google starts with technology and ends with the user, bringing both together in unconventional ways. Our job is to demonstrate how Google's products solve the world's problems--from the everyday to the epic, from the mundane to the monumental. And we approach marketing in a way that only Google can--changing the game, redefining the medium, making the user the priority, and ultimately, letting the technology speak for itself. The US base salary range for this full-time position is $162,000-$240,000 + bonus + equity + benefits. Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Within the range, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your preferred location during the hiring process. Please note that the compensation details listed in US role postings reflect the base salary only, and do not include bonus, equity, or benefits. Learn more about benefits at Google . Responsibilities Work with CAM S&O’s Executive Director to deliver and execute strategy for Consumer Apps Marketing’s AI transformation, identifying and prioritizing core business areas with the greatest potential for AI-driven disruption. Develop the roadmap for transformation, articulating a compelling goal of the future state and quantifying expected Profit and Loss (P&L) impact through increased productivity and reduced operational cost. Own the organization Objectives and Key Results (OKR) to rapidly increase our Marketers’ AI proficiency of Google’s tools, continuously finding ways to educate and surface new possibilities into workflows. Own the goal, strategy, and execution across our various Marketing functions for their AI transformation, rethinking processes by using AI to replace legacy workflows and improve coordination. Serve as the primary operator for horizontal projects across CAM, rapidly analyzing ambiguous, high-stakes problems and delivering clear, actionable recommendations. description: SMBC Group is a top-tier global financial group. Headquartered in Tokyo and with a 400-year history, SMBC Group offers a diverse range of financial services, including banking, leasing, securities, credit cards, and consumer finance. (SMFG) is the holding company of SMBC Group, which is one of the three largest banking groups in Japan. SMFG’s shares trade on the Tokyo, Nagoya, and New York (NYSE: SMFG) stock exchanges. Develop strong relationships across all functional areas including Risk, Compliance, Legal, Audit, and HR. Manage team of product managers and designers Strong knowledge of market trends and emerging innovations within the cash management arena. P&L ownership across managed product offerings. title: Commercial Strategy/Ops Lead, Global Studio, XCM
description: DESCRIPTION Are you passionate about driving financial strategy and operational excellence at scale? At XCM’s Global Studio team, we are looking for a strategic Commercial Operations leader to support the growth phase of Global Studio with strong commercial rigor. As the Commercial Strategy & Operations Lead for Global Studio, you'll be responsible for managing end-to-end financial operations, developing and implementing chargeback models, and ensuring operational efficiency across our global creative production platform. You'll work closely with cross-functional teams to drive financial planning, cost optimization, and sustainable growth. Key job responsibilities Financial Strategy & Operations: • Own end-to-end financial operations including rate card finalization, implementation and governance • Drive the development and implementation of the chargeback model across multiple businesses • Manage budget tracking, forecasting, and variance analysis • Lead rate reviews, negotiations and financial planning with agency partners Support financial analysis for expansion decisions and efficiency initiatives Strategic Planning & Business Operations: Partner with XCM Finance on cost allocation methodologies Drive towards and maintain a cost steady state of Global Studio Develop and optimize operational frameworks for financial tracking and reporting Create and implement financial governance models across different business units Lead quarterly and annual financial planning processes Stakeholder Management & Reporting: Build and maintain relationships with key finance stakeholders across organizations Create regular financial performance reports and analyses Present financial insights and recommendations to leadership Drive alignment on financial methodologies across different business units About the team Global Studio is Amazon's comprehensive in-house creative production service, designed to replace fragmented agency relationships and manage creative production needs for Amazon teams globally. It extends internal creative teams’ capabilities by combining XCM's creative excellence with AI-powered automation. This gives teams the best of both worlds - the control and governance of an in-house team, and the flexibility, efficiency, and diverse talent of a global external partner. Global Studio's implementation is critical and timely as we face unprecedented challenges and opportunities in the digital marketing landscape and pressures on marketing teams. In today's digital-first environment, our ability to deliver high-quality, contextually relevant creative content at scale has become a critical competitive differentiator. By addressing these pressing challenges now, Global Studio positions Amazon to lead in marketing effectiveness and efficiency, driving better business outcomes and customer experiences in an increasingly complex digital landscape. Global Studio isn't just another tool or process change – it's a fundamental reimagining of how we produce creative at Amazon. Imagine being able to focus on strategic campaign planning instead of vendor management, or having the confidence that your assets will maintain consistent quality, agnostic of the locale or team. Our vision is to be a one-stop shop for creative needs across Amazon, while removing the constraint of high-quality, on-brand creative asset availability. BASIC QUALIFICATIONS 6+ years of professional non-internship marketing experience Experience building, executing and scaling cross-functional marketing programs Experience using Microsoft Excel to manipulate and analyze data Experience using data and metrics to measure impact and determine improvements Experience communicating results to senior leadership Experience communicating results to stakeholders PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS Experience with Excel or Tableau (data manipulation, macros, charts and pivot tables) Experience in creative operations or agency financial management Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status. title: Analyst - Structuring, Capital Markets & Strategy
description: About Global Atlantic Global Atlantic is a leading provider of retirement security and investment solutions with operations in the U.S., Bermuda, and Japan. As a wholly-owned subsidiary of KKR (NYSE: KKR), a leading global investment firm, Global Atlantic combines deep insurance expertise with KKR’s powerful investment capabilities to deliver long-term financial security for millions of individuals worldwide. With a broad suite of annuity, preneed life insurance, reinsurance, and investment solutions, Global Atlantic, through its issuing companies, helps people achieve their financial goals with confidence. For more information, please visit www.globalatlantic.com. KKR is a leading global investment firm that offers alternative asset management as well as capital markets and insurance solutions. KKR aims to generate attractive investment returns by following a patient and disciplined investment approach, employing world-class people, and supporting growth in its portfolio companies and communities. KKR sponsors investment funds that invest in private equity, credit and real assets and has strategic partners that manage hedge funds. KKR’s insurance subsidiaries offer retirement, life and reinsurance products under the management of Global Atlantic Financial Group. References to KKR’s investments may include the activities of its sponsored funds and insurance subsidiaries. The Analyst will join the New York based Global Atlantic Investment Office as part of our Structuring, Capital Markets and Strategy team, with a focus on structuring, deal execution and transaction management. Responsibilities Include Work with portfolio managers to develop and model debt instruments including ABS/CLO securitizations, project & corporate finance structures, and other debt solutions that optimize capital, accounting and tax on assets Global Atlantic acquires Support transaction execution process from beginning to end, including amongst others: preparation and review of legal documentation with external counsel managing workstreams with external stakeholders including but not limited to trustees, servicers, rating agencies, and accountants liaising with internal stakeholders such as accounting, tax, middle office, and analytics teams throughout the transaction process Support the Structuring, Capital Markets and Strategy team by ensuring transactions are operating in accordance with deal documentation. Assist with deal re-structures and re-ratings for existing transactions and interface with deal counsel to ensure timely and successful deal closings. Bachelor’s Degree Required Proficiency in Excel, PowerPoint, and structured finance modeling tools (Intex DealMaker and IntexCalc) Individuals with strong verbal and written communication skills and is highly organized and attentive to detail Strong leadership and team orientation; organized and high attention to detail Flexible individuals who are both creative and commercial and are comfortable working on multiple transactions at once across a variety of assets Energetic, inquisitive and a high initiative to take on projects To be considered for this role, applicants must be authorized to work for any employer in the U.S. We are unable to sponsor or assume sponsorship of an employment Visa at this time. title: Data Product Owner
description: You’re more valuable than ever – And that’s just how we’ll make you feel. Job Summary The Data Product Owner is responsible for leading the development and delivery of data products that support business strategy and decision-making. This role collaborates with cross-functional teams—including stakeholders, data engineers, analysts, and business units—to define product vision, manage the product backlog, and ensure execution of data initiatives aligned with organizational goals and user needs. Qualifications Education Required: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Information Technology, Business, or a related field. Work Experience Required: 3+ years’ experience as a Product Owner, Product Manager, or similar role in a data-driven environment. Strong background in data engineering, data analytics, or data science. Experience working with cloud platforms (e.g., AWS, Google Cloud, Snowflake) and data tools (e.g., SQL, ETL, BI tools). Preferred Qualifications, Education, Licenses, Certifications, Experience, etc.: Experience in data modeling, API management, and machine learning would be advantageous. Certified Scrum Product Owner (CSPO) or equivalent Agile Product Owner certification. Certified Product Manager (CPM) or Pragmatic Institute Certification. DAMA Certified Data Management Professional (CDMP) or equivalent data governance/data management certification. Tableau Desktop Specialist or Tableau Certified Data Analyst. dbt Fundamentals or dbt Advanced Certification. Snowflake SnowPro Core Certification. Certification in Agile (e.g., Certified SAFe Product Owner/Product Manager). Experience working with data products or platforms like Snowflake, BigQuery, or Azure Data Lake. A Master’s degree is a plus Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities This role involves interaction and collaboration with other departments and requires excellent judgment and interpersonal skills. Strong understanding of Agile methodologies with experience managing product backlogs using tools such as Jira, Azure DevOps, or similar platforms. Excellent communication and leadership skills, with the ability to translate complex technical concepts into clear business language. Analytical mindset with the ability to interpret complex data and make informed, data driven decisions. Knowledge of data governance, data security, and compliance best practices. Proven ability to manage stakeholders and collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams. Essential Duties and Responsibilities Product Strategy & Vision Define and communicate the vision and roadmap for data products, ensuring alignment with business goals and stakeholder needs. Collaborate with stakeholders to understand business objectives, user requirements, and product opportunities. Prioritize and manage the data product backlog in alignment with organizational goals, ensuring that all features, enhancements, and technical improvements are documented, approved, and scheduled in accordance with established processes. Translate approved business requirements into clear, detailed user stories and acceptance criteria that provide actionable direction for technical teams. Collaborate with Data Engineering, Business Intelligence, and Data Governance teams to maintain, refine, and update the backlog, ensuring alignment with regulatory, operational, and security standards. Ensure backlog items are accurately documented, appropriately prioritized, and properly estimated, in compliance with Agile delivery practices and internal change management procedures. Support sprint planning and release management activities by confirming that all backlog items meet defined readiness criteria prior to development. Maintain records of backlog prioritization decisions and stakeholder approvals in accordance with GoHealth Urgent Care policies and applicable data governance frameworks. Stakeholder Engagement Act as the primary liaison between business stakeholders and technical teams to ensure alignment and transparency Facilitate regular stakeholder meetings to gather feedback, resolve issues, and adjust the data product roadmap Development Oversight Guide cross-functional teams through the data product development lifecycle, from concept to delivery Ensure data products are built to specification, meet quality standards, and are delivered on time Collaborate on acceptance criteria and review completed work to confirm it meets the definition of done Data Governance & Quality Ensure data products maintain integrity, accuracy, and accessibility Support the development and implementation of data governance frameworks and policies Market & User Research Conduct market and competitive analysis to identify opportunities and gaps in data products Engage with end users to gather insights, identify pain points, and validate product value Product Metrics & Performance Define and monitor KPIs to evaluate product performance and drive continuous improvement Present performance insights to leadership, highlighting key metrics, risks, and opportunities Innovation & Improvement Stay current with • Stay current with industry trends in data, analytics, and technology Recommend new tools, technologies, and methodologies to enhance data product capabilities industry trends in data, analytics, and technology Recommend new tools, technologies, and methodologies to enhance data product capabilities GoHealth Core Values GoHealth’s Core Values, listed below, are essential functions of this position: Collaboration: Takes ownership for collectively establishing productive partnerships and relationships and seek to gain joint understanding of priorities and objectives so that the greater good of the organization and those we serve is always at the forefront. Innovation: Consistently uses good judgment, applying creativity to overcome obstacles and increasing effectiveness and efficiency through process and other forms of innovation. Engages the strengths and talents of each GoHealth team member, creating an environment of involvement, respect and connection where the richness of ideas, backgrounds and perspectives are harnessed. Courage & Integrity: Models and practices the highest ethical and professional standards; demonstrates pride and personal interest in our patients, partners and fellow team members, deeply engaging in the business. Makes decisions, with a focus on doing the right thing; treats team members, our customers, partners and vendors with dignity, consideration, open-mindedness and respect. Accountability: Always shows initiative, demonstrates a bias to action and gets things done. Actively accepts responsibility for diverse roles, obligations and actions that positively influence patient and customer outcomes, our partnerships and the healthcare needs of our communities. Physical Requirements Office Environment: Tasks may be conducted within a climate-controlled office setting. Physical Activity: The role may require the ability to lift, carry, push, or pull materials, supplies, and equipment (up to 25 lbs.). Duties typically involve a combination of sitting, standing, and walking, with frequent changes in position. Travel: Travel may be required, including travel between Company locations and out-oftown destinations as needed. Up to 10%. Safety Equipment: May require the use of safety equipment for infection prevention. _____________________ Note: This Job Description is not inclusive of all the duties of the position. You may be asked by Leaders to perform other duties. Leadership may revise this job description at any time. Set up email alerts as new job postings become available that meet your interest! title: Analyst II, Finance- Strategy
description: 8901 - Corp Office West Crk - 12800 Tuckahoe Creek Parkway, Richmond, Virginia, 23238 CarMax, the way your career should be! The Expansion Planning & Analysis team is a community of analysts with a variety of strategic and technical skillsets. We work together to develop data-driven expansion strategies and guide CarMax to make sound financial investments that enable our growth goals, particularly around retail sales, vehicle reconditioning, and wholesale auctions. Our team tackles these complex problems while striving to be a great representation of CarMax’s 4 core values: Do the Right Thing, Put People First, Win Together, and Go for Greatness About The Role CarMax relies on significant investments in physical infrastructure to fuel our future growth. These investments include both the continued expansion of our retail locations as well as the vast network of facilities required to support sales of 1,600,000+ vehicle a year with reconditioning, auction, and transportation capacity. Our team of business analysts plays a critical role in identifying future opportunities for expansion, developing strategies to support growth on a 3-10 year time horizon, and highlighting key insights to unlock optimal returns on our existing investment portfolio. Key Questions Include Where are the largest opportunity areas to reach new customers? What store type is optimal for serving that demand? What is the expected return on investment? How should future investments change as more of our business shifts online? Which regions will need additional reconditioning and auction facilities? How should these facilities be prioritized and when is the optimal time to build? As an Analyst, you will work closely with our Operational and Strategy business partners to make key recommendations for financially optimal expansion strategies. These strategies will support CarMax’s long term strategic goals and ensure that we have the right infrastructure in place to meet the growing needs of our retail and wholesale customers. What You Will Do – Essential Responsibilities A successful finance strategy analyst will: Be an analytic partner and establish a close working relationship with our business partners Conduct data and financial analysis to propose and evaluate future store growth and expansion opportunities Enhance and utilize analytical tools (models, reports, dashboards) to monitor store performance and identify opportunities to drive innovation and efficiency Translate insights into clear business recommendations for operational partners and department leadership Qualifications And Requirements Ability to consistently deliver at a high level on the responsibilities listed above. Requirements listed below are representative of the knowledge and skills required: Four-year undergraduate degree with strong academic record 2+ years experience in a finance, analytical or strategic role strongly preferred Ability to travel and perform site visits to prospective & existing CarMax locations as needed Experience conducting data and financial analysis to support conclusions, and a willingness to gain proficiency in data analysis tools such as SQL or Tableau Ability to communicate complex topics to people with varying backgrounds and levels of technical familiarity Enthusiasm for collaborating with a team of passionate analysts and business partners who regularly shape both big picture growth strategy and tactical site-specific investment decisions Work Location and Arrangement: This role will be based out of the CarMax Home Office in Richmond, VA and have a Hybrid work arrangement. Associates based in Richmond work onsite 5 days per week. Work Authorization:  Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis. Sponsorship will not be considered for this specific role. About CarMax CarMax disrupted the auto industry by delivering the honest, transparent and high-integrity experience customers want and deserve. This innovative thinking around the way cars are bought and sold has helped us become the nation’s largest retailer of used cars, with over 250 locations nationwide. Our amazing team of more than 25,000 associates work together to deliver iconic customer experiences. Along the way, we help every associate grow their career and achieve their best, at work and in their community.  We are recognized for our commitment to training and diversity and are one of the FORTUNE 100 Best Companies to Work For®. Our Commitment To Diversity And Inclusion CarMax is committed to bringing together people from different backgrounds and perspectives, providing employees with a safe, welcoming, and inclusive work environment. CarMax is an equal opportunity employer, and all qualified candidates will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, genetic information, national origin, protected veteran status, disability status, or any other characteristic protected by law. Upon an applicant's request, CarMax will consider reasonable accommodation to complete the CarMax Job Application.

title: Senior UX Content Designer
description: Description A portfolio demonstrating relevant UX design work is required. Candidates should be prepared to provide a link or copy upon request. Your Role We are seeking a highly experienced Senior Content Designer to craft clear, consistent, and inclusive product content that helps our customers accomplish their goals with confidence. In this role, you’ll define and deliver the language and content structures that make our digital experiences usable, trustworthy, and on-brand. You will partner closely with product managers, designers, researchers, engineers, legal, and brand teams to turn complex problems into simple, empathetic guidance—anchored in evidence and aligned to business outcomes. You will operate at both the systems and journey levels, shaping voice and tone guidelines, reusable content patterns, nomenclature, and taxonomy that scale across brands and products. At this level, driving alignment across teams of teams and influencing strategic direction through insight, narrative, and measurable impact will be core to your success. We support product teams building configurable features and capabilities that continually enhance the end-user experience and business performance. We do this by: Understanding the motivations, behaviors, and unmet needs of our millions of customers—and translating those insights into clear, helpful, and humane content Collaborating with business, brand, and technology partners to align platforms, capabilities, and processes to a shared content vision Advocating for clarity, coherence, and continuity across the journey—prioritizing learnability, efficiency, memorability, and desirability Operating within a Lean/Agile approach that allows us to validate assumptions, launch incrementally, and learn quickly In addition, how we define and govern content is as important as what we publish. Content Designers are expected to advocate for, and contribute to, a systems-based methodology that supports configurability, customization, and specialization of reusable content components—enabling scalability, consistency, and quality across multi-tenant implementations. Why You Belong Here At Victoria’s Secret & Co, you’ll join a world-leading specialty retail brand recognized globally for innovation and excellence in lingerie and fashion. You’ll work alongside industry leaders to set the standard for what a retail brand can achieve, placing customers at the center of everything we do to create products and experiences that bring them joy. We believe everyone deserves a place where they truly belong. We celebrate individuality and know that your passion, experience, and unique perspective strengthen our team and business. Here, you’ll be empowered to perform, grow, and engage through unmatched opportunities to develop your skills, gain real-world experience, and learn from the best in the business. Your Impact Serve as a strategic content partner and subject matter expert for a brand, product area, platform, or experience domain Define and maintain voice & tone guidelines, content principles, and editorial standards that ensure clarity, consistency, and brand alignment across surfaces Create and optimize UX copy across flows (navigation, onboarding, forms, CTAs, error messages, notifications, transactional emails/SMS, empty states, tooltips, and help content) Develop reusable content patterns and nomenclature within our design system (e.g., error frameworks, form field guidance, CTA taxonomy, naming conventions) to support multi-tenant, configurable implementations Partner with Research to shape hypotheses, test content, synthesize insights (comprehension, readability, sentiment), and evolve strategies based on evidence Use data and analytics (conversion, task success, search behavior, readability) to evaluate content effectiveness and refine direction; define content-specific success metrics Collaborate with UX Design, Front-End and Back-End Engineering, Legal, and Brand to ensure content decisions are feasible, compliant, and consistent with platform capabilities Lead cross-functional workshops to clarify problem spaces, align on terminology, and establish shared definitions of success Define and steward content models, IA, taxonomy, and metadata that improve findability, comprehension, and cross-brand coherence Champion accessibility and inclusion: plain language, WCAG-aligned content, semantic structure, screen-reader support, alt text, localization readiness, and inclusive writing practices Partner with marketing and SEO specialists to ensure canonical naming, structured data alignment, and ethical content practices (no dark patterns) Present strategic narratives and recommendations to cross-functional partners—clearly communicating rationale, tradeoffs, and expected impact Participate in roadmap planning to align experience and content strategy with user needs and business priorities Mentor and influence teams by elevating critique, sharing frameworks and case studies, contributing to team guidelines and our blog, and continuously improving how we work. Click here for benefit details related to this position. Minimum Salary: $99,400.00 Maximum Salary: $134,715.00 VS&Co provides a range of compensation for this role as shown. Your actual salary will be determined by a number of factors, including: your specific skills and experience, geographic region, or other relevant factors. Qualifications Passionate advocate for users; expert at translating complex concepts into plain, purposeful language Deep experience with UX writing, content strategy, systems thinking, IA, taxonomy, and editorial governance Strong storytelling, facilitation, and alignment-building skills; comfort navigating ambiguity and framing complex problem spaces Collaborative, inclusive, and influential working style; able to partner across product, design, engineering, legal, brand, and marketing Data-literate and outcomes-oriented; experienced in defining content metrics and iterating based on qualitative and quantitative insight Strong understanding of digital commerce, usability best practices, and the role of content in conversion, retention, and support Accessibility and localization expertise; familiarity with WCAG, semantic HTML, and assistive technologies Highly organized with excellent attention to detail and follow-through; positive, constructive approach Bachelor’s Degree in English, Journalism, Communications, HCI, Linguistics, Psychology, or equivalent professional experience 5+ years in Content Design/UX Writing/Editorial roles on digital products (e-commerce strongly preferred) Demonstrated impact at systems and journey levels, including content guidelines, pattern libraries, nomenclature, and taxonomy work Portfolio showcasing end-to-end flows, content strategy artifacts, and measurable outcomes (e.g., comprehension, conversion, task success) Experience collaborating in Agile environments with researchers, designers, PMs, and engineers Proficiency with tools such as figma, CMS platforms), analytics experimentation tools and localization workflows Understanding of front-end semantics (HTML/ARIA), SEO fundamentals, and structured content practices Retail/fashion brand experience preferred; e-commerce experience ideal History of delivering business and customer outcomes through content strategy and UX writing A portfolio demonstrating relevant UX design work is required. Candidates should be prepared to provide a link or copy upon request.* We will consider for employment all qualified applicants, including those with arrest records, conviction records, or other criminal histories, in a manner consistent with the requirements of any applicable state and local laws. Please see links: California Fair Chance Act, Los Angeles Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring Ordinance, Philadelphia Fair Chance Law, San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance An equal opportunity employer, we do not discriminate in hiring or terms and conditions of employment because of an individual’s race, color, religion, gender, gender identity, national origin, citizenship, age, disability, sexual orientation, marital status or any other protected category recognized by state, federal or local laws. We only hire individuals authorized for employment in the United States.

title: Technical Product Manager
description: Job Summary Our mission is to leverage creative technology in the oil and gas industry to eliminate emissions, increase production, and move us toward a more sustainable future. We enable that mission by designing, building, and evolving the technical platforms that stream, process, structure, and report on data across our vertically integrated products. Our Technical teams work across systems, platforms, and data to ensure our technology scales, performs, and delivers measurable outcomes for the business and the environment. Are you energized by solving complex technical problems through collaboration and thoughtful design? Do you enjoy working at the intersection of product intent and technical execution? Are you curious about how systems fit together, how data flows through platforms, and how technical decisions shape user experience and reporting outcomes? Do you thrive in environments where learning, iteration, and continuous improvement are expected? Are you excited to contribute to a mission-driven organization where technology is a core driver of impact? Join the technical team at FlowCo and help shape the platforms that power our products. This role is for a Technical Product Manager who is motivated by understanding systems, translating product needs into technical solutions, and partnering with cross-functional technical teams to deliver meaningful outcomes. In this role, you will work closely with Software, Data, and Design partners to define requirements, prioritize work, and ensure solutions align with product standards, user experience expectations, and reporting needs. The Technical organization at FlowCo is a growing, product-focused group responsible for the platforms and systems that enable our business. Our teams span software, data, quality, and analytics disciplines, working together to deliver reliable, scalable, and impactful solutions. If you’re looking to begin your career in a role that blends product thinking with technical problem-solving — and want to contribute to technology that makes a real-world environmental impact — FlowCo is the place for you Responsibilities Drive technical solutions with empathy for internal and external users Translate product and business requirements into clear technical epics, user stories, and acceptance criteria Partner with Software and Data teams across the full stack to understand system behavior, constraints, and solution options Collaborate with product designers to ensure user experience, interfaces, and workflows align with product design standards and reporting requirements Support the delivery of software features and platform capabilities for assigned product areas, with increasing ownership over time Maintain and prioritize technical backlogs based on business value, delivery sequencing, and technical considerations, with guidance from senior product and technical leaders Participate in technical and design discussions to help surface trade-offs, dependencies, and risks Validate delivered work against acceptance criteria and support defect triage and resolution Communicate clearly and effectively with Software, Data, Design, Product, and business stakeholders Leverage Jira and Confluence to track work items, document requirements, and support agile ceremonies Deliver features and initiatives with mentorship and oversight, growing toward greater autonomy and scope Responsibilities Required Bachelor’s degree or equivalent practical experience Internship or 1+ years of experience in a product, technical, engineering-adjacent, or systems-focused role Exposure to agile or iterative software delivery environments (Scrum, Kanban, or similar) Foundational understanding of how modern software systems and data-driven products are built Ability to translate business or user needs into clear, actionable work for technical teams Strong written and verbal communication skills Experience using Jira, Confluence, or similar work-tracking and documentation tools Preferred Experience working with cross-functional Software, Data, and Design teams Familiarity with full-stack application concepts (front-end, services, data, reporting) Understanding of UX principles and how design standards influence implementation Exposure to analytics, reporting, or data-informed product features Product Owner, Scrum, or related certifications

title: Product Manager
description: Beacon Hill Technologies is seeking a successful hands-on Product Manager / Product Owner (dual role) functioning as a cross-program lead across multiple related initiatives. The role operates as a "jack of all trades," blending Product Management, Product Ownership, and delivery coordination. The individual will initially focus on learning and discovery, then transition into driving build execution, including potential build-vs-buy decisions and coordination of internal and third-party development teams. Core Responsibilities Product Management & Delivery Own and refine epics, user stories, acceptance criteria, and backlog prioritization for pricing, costing, and billing capabilities Support sprint planning, backlog grooming, release planning, and stakeholder demos Translate complex business and data requirements into clear product artifacts Document decisions, tradeoffs, and requirements Communicate effectively with business, technical teams, and executive stakeholders Cross-Program Integration Coordinate work across multiple initiatives and vendor streams (e.g., ServiceNow CPQ, analytics partners, internal platform teams) Map and align end-to-end processes (pricing → costing → billing) Ensure consistent data and workflow outcomes across systems Governance & SDLC Discipline Ensure compliance with internal ISO and SDLC standards Drive structured documentation and disciplined delivery practices Deliver scalable, production-ready enterprise solutions Stakeholder Leadership Partner with IT, Engineering leadership, and Business Analysts Facilitate working sessions and key decisions Provide concise written and verbal executive updates Beacon Hill is an equal opportunity employer and individuals with disabilities and/or protected veterans are encouraged to apply. This form will be used for reporting purposes only and will be kept separate from all other records. title: Generative AI Product Owner
description: We’re Watts. Together, we’re reimagining the future of water. We feel proud every day about what we do. We're all part of the same crucial mission, no matter what function we support - it's to provide safe, clean water for the world, and to protect our planet's most valuable resource. What we do: For 150 years, Watts has built best-in-class products that are trusted by customers in residential and commercial settings across the world. We are at the forefront of innovation, working with cutting-edge technology to provide smart and connected, sustainable water solutions for the future. Watts is a leading brand with a quality reputation — and we have a dynamic future ahead. Scope of Position Reporting to the VP, Intelligent Process Automation, the Generative AI Product Owner is responsible for defining, delivering, and continuously improving enterprise-grade Generative AI capabilities, with an initial and primary focus on Microsoft Copilot across M365, Power Platform, and integrated business applications with a consideration for SAP Joule, BTP, and S/4 HANA. This role sits at the intersection of business value, user experience, and technology execution. The Product Owner will translate business needs into a clear GenAI product roadmap, prioritize high-value use cases, and partner closely with IT, Security, Data, Legal, and Business leaders to ensure Copilot and Joule are deployed responsibly, securely, and at scale. The role emphasizes value realization, adoption, and governance, not experimentation for its own sake. This position is hybrid and based in Andover, MA. Primary Job Duties and Responsibilities Own the Generative AI product vision and roadmap, with primary focus on Microsoft Copilot across M365 and Power Platform along with developments in SAP Joule Define and track measurable outcomes tied to productivity, cost reduction, and business enablement. Identify and prioritize high-value GenAI use cases across enterprise functions. Lead enterprise rollout and adoption of AI Solutions, ensuring they are embedded into real business workflows. Conduct training and workshops on copilot and other power applications to drive personal productivity. Educate end users on compliance and governance. Own and prioritize the GenAI product backlog, translating business needs into clear, secure user stories. Partner with engineering and platform teams to deliver GenAI capabilities using agile, incremental delivery. Ensure all GenAI solutions comply with AI governance, security, privacy, and Responsible AI standards. Act as the primary GenAI product interface between IT, business leaders, and executive stakeholders. Required Qualifications 5+ years of experience as a Product Owner, Product Manager, or similar role in IT or digital platforms. Hands-on experience delivering Microsoft and SAP ecosystem solutions (M365, Power Platform, Azure, Joule, BTP, S/4 HANA). Practical exposure to Generative AI tools, preferably Microsoft Copilot or similar enterprise AI platforms. Experience working in regulated or enterprise environments with strong security and governance requirements. Strong understanding of: Agile product delivery and backlog management Enterprise productivity tools and workflows Data privacy, security, and AI risk considerations Ability to translate business problems into product features and measurable outcomes. Preferred Qualifications Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills. Experience with Microsoft Copilot Studio, Power Automate, Power Apps, or Azure OpenAI or SAP Joule, BTP, ABAP. Familiarity with Responsible AI frameworks and AI governance models. Experience driving technology adoption and change at scale. Background in manufacturing, industrial, or B2B environments. General Applicable Company Competencies Commitment to Watts’ values of integrity, accountability, continuous improvement and innovation, and transparency. Punctuality and dependability. Ability to be flexible and adapt to changing work priorities and stressful conditions. Adherence to all personnel policies, procedures, and standards of process as implemented by Watts. Maintain productive and collaborative relationships with other Watts employees. Adherence to Watts’ seven cultural beliefs: Growth Mindset, Customer-Focused Innovation, Constant Communication, Clear Goals, Collaborate Globally, Be Inclusive, and Take Action. Working Conditions: While performing the job duties, you will be working in an office environment. You will be required to work in the office at the Andover, MA location three days per week (Monday – Wednesday) and can work remotely two days per week (Thursday and Friday). Physical Requirements : Specific physical abilities required for this position include, but are not limited to: Ability to remain seated at a desk or workstation for extended periods. Ability to perform repetitive tasks like typing on a keyboard or using a mouse for extended periods. Ability to physically move around the office, organize or transport files, packages, or other office-related materials. Ability to read documents, use a computer, and perform data entry tasks. Ability to communicate clearly with management and coworkers, particularly in meetings or phone calls. Ability to operate standard office equipment such as computers, printers, phones, and copiers. Ability to occasionally lift and carry light objects, such as office supplies, documents, or small equipment. Pay Range : The expected salary range for this position is $132,000- $153,000 yearly. Actual compensation will be dependent upon individual skills, experience, qualifications, and applicable law. Nothing in this job description restricts Watts’ right to assign or reassign duties, responsibilities, and working hours/conditions to this position at any time. This position is “at will,” which means that either the employee or Watts may terminate the employment relationship at any time, with or without notice, and for any lawful reason. Watts in it for you : Please note that the following benefits apply only to permanent roles and do not apply to internship roles. Competitive compensation based on your skills, qualifications and experience Comprehensive medical and dental coverage, retirement benefits Family building benefits, including paid maternity/paternity leave 10 paid holidays and Paid Time Off Continued professional development opportunities and educational reimbursement Additional perks such as fitness reimbursements and employee discount programs Learn more about our benefit offerings here: https://tapintowattsbenefits.com/ How we work: At Watts, our culture is team-oriented and supportive. Employees here genuinely care about the quality of their work, and about each other. Our people are the heart of who we are and contribute to our longevity and continued success. And this is a place where you can have a big career. No matter your role, there are opportunities for learning and development, and your daily contributions make a meaningful impact on the lives of people who use our products and on the future of water. Watts is committed to equal employment opportunity. We follow a policy of administering all employment decisions and personnel actions without regard to race, color, religion, creed, sex, pregnancy, national origin, sexual orientation, age, physical or mental disability, genetic disposition or carrier status, marital status, military or veteran status, minorities, or any other category protected under applicable federal, state, or local law. Consistent with the obligations of state and federal law, Watts will make reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities. Any employee who needs a reasonable accommodation should contact Human Resources.

title: Product Manager - AI & Data Analytics
description: We are seeking a results-oriented Product Manager to drive the vision and execution of our customer-facing SaaS products, data-driven features, and AI/ML solutions. This role is a critical bridge, translating deep technical capabilities from data science and engineering into clear, quantifiable business value for our B2B SaaS customers. This is a highly visible, customer-facing role that involves working directly with key clients to co-develop innovative solutions. The ideal candidate is a curious, data-minded problem-solver who is excited to bridge the gap between business needs and technical solutions. This is a growth role where you will have a major impact on our product and our customers. WHAT YOU'LL DO: Product Strategy & Vision Own the product vision and strategic roadmap for our external, customer-facing AI/ML models, data products, and analytics features. Engage directly with strategic customers and design partners to understand their needs, validate roadmaps, and co-develop new features. Define, measure, and communicate product success using metrics that correlate with customer value and SaaS business metrics (e.g., adoption, retention, ARR expansion). Partner with UX, Data Science, and Sales Engineering to create impactful data visualizations and product demos that communicate clear value to customers. Product Execution & Delivery Define clear, testable requirements (epics and user stories) based on customer feedback, market analysis, and strategic priorities. Write precise, detailed specifications for complex data processes, transformation logic, and aggregation rules. Partner with Product Marketing, Sales, and Customer Success to develop launch plans, sales collateral, and communication plans for new features. Technical Analysis & Enablement Use your analytical skills to conduct product-related data analysis, validate data assets, and prove/disprove hypotheses. Act as the primary translator between customer requirements and data engineering, using your technical fluency to assess the viability of new ideas. Work closely with data scientists to define requirements for AI/ML-driven features, ensuring the underlying data is structured for success. WHO YOU ARE: We know that great talent comes from many backgrounds. Whether you fit one of these descriptions or have a completely different story, if you're a data-driven problem-solver, we want to hear from you. The Data PM: You're already a Product Manager working on data products. You're looking for a new challenge in a customer-facing role where you can have more impact and co-develop solutions on a modern data stack directly with clients. The Data-Driven PM: You're a successful PM for a different SaaS product, but you find yourself spending all your time in the data, building your own dashboards, and talking to the analytics team. You're ready to make data and AI your full-time focus. The Product-Minded Analyst: You're a Senior Data Analyst or BI Developer. You're the one your stakeholders always come to for "what's next," not just "what happened." You've been "unofficially" product-managing your own work and are ready to make the leap into a formal product role. WHAT YOU'LL BRING: Experience: 3-5+ years of relevant experience in one of the fields described above. Product & Business Acumen: A strong ability to connect complex technical features directly to customer value, user needs, and business outcomes (like ROI or retention). Customer Collaboration: Experience working directly with customers or key stakeholders to gather requirements, validate ideas, and manage feedback loops. Communication & Stakeholder Management: Excellent communication and data storytelling skills. You can build consensus and manage expectations with diverse audiences (from engineering to sales to external clients). Prioritization & Planning: A demonstrated ability to manage a backlog, make trade-offs, and align teams on a clear set of priorities. SQL Proficiency: You have solid SQL fundamentals and are comfortable writing queries (like joins and aggregations) to explore data and conduct analysis. We're more interested in your analytical problem-solving skills than your memorization of advanced syntax. BI Tool Proficiency: Experience with a modern BI platform (e.g., Tableau, Looker, Power BI, Sigma). You understand the principles of effective data visualization. Data Fluency: A strong functional understanding of data transformation techniques (aggregation, string manipulation, type casting). WHAT WOULD ALSO BE AWESOME: Modern Data Stack Experience: This is a great opportunity to work with (or learn) industry-leading tools. Our stack includes Snowflake, Fivetran, DBT, and Sigma. Domain Expertise: Experience with B2B SaaS verticals such as Field Services (e.g., pest control, lawn care) or Building Services (e.g., cleaning, security) would give you an advantage in onboarding speed. Scripting Familiarity: You have some familiarity with a scripting language (like R or Python) for data analysis. AI/ML Experience: You have exposure to the AI/ML lifecycle or have worked with production machine learning models. In our commitment to salary transparency, we provide a compensation range for each role. In addition to the base salary this role is bonus eligible, the final offer will be dependent on various factors, including the candidate's qualifications, relevant experience, and the organization's budget. Our Talent Acquisition team will provide more information about the compensation package for this position during the interview process. Please note that salary estimates provided by websites (LinkedIn, Glassdoor, etc.) and not by WorkWave may not accurately reflect the actual salary range for the position.WHAT YOU SHOULD KNOW ABOUT US: We are laid back but buttoned up. We offer a casual work environment and remote work flexibility and have a passion for developing creative, innovative best in class solutions that directly contribute to the success of our customers We care deeply and deliver service and solutions that make a real difference in the lives of our clients and their businesses We openly accept others as they are and build strong partnerships based on trust Teamwork and collaboration is key to help our colleagues and customers solve their challenges Our team is energetic, fun, naturally inquisitive and eager to make an impact, we invite you to join us! LOVE WHAT YOU DO, NO MATTER WHERE YOU DO IT: Join our Remote-First Global Work Community: WorkWave provides an innovative and dynamic remote-first Global Work Community that encourages growth, creativity, and collaboration. No matter what stage of your career or where you live, WorkWave is your place to be part of a global company with a startup feel, where your ideas matter and your growth is a priority. A GLOBAL COMPANY WITH A LOCAL PRESENCE: We know that there are benefits of being in the office and working from home. WorkWave promotes a healthy work/life balance and provides employees with the flexibility of collaborating in the office or the option to work virtually if desired. Our teams are well versed at working collaboratively in a fully virtual environment. Our HQ is based at our state of the art home office in the historic Bell Works complex located in Holmdel Township, New Jersey. We keep our offices available to all to use when working remotely isn’t feasible, or to help with cross training, team building and/or brainstorming. We have employees in over 30 states, 7 countries and many regional offices - each with their own set of perks and opportunities to give back to the local community. title: Sr. Product Development Manager, Professional Education
description: Overview Since our founding in 1924, we've cut cardiovascular disease deaths in half, but there is still so much more to do. To overcome today’s biggest health challenges and accelerate this progress, we need passionate individuals like you. Join our movement, be part of the progress, and help ensure a healthier future for all. You matter, and so does the impact you can make with us. The American Heart Association has an excellent opportunity for a Sr. Product Development Manager, Professional Education! This position can be home-based. This is a full time, benefits eligible, grant funded opportunity. Current funding will expire on June 30, 2027. The Association offers many resources to help you maintain work-life harmonization through your changing needs and life situations. To help you be successful, you will have access to Heart U, our award-winning corporate university, as well as additional training and support, locally. \#TheAHALife is more than a company culture; it is our way of life. It embodies our commitment to work-life harmonization and is guided by our core values where our employees can thrive both personally and professionally. Discover why you will Be Seen. Be Heard. Be Valued at the American Heart Association by following us on LinkedIn, Instagram, Facebook, X, and at heart.jobs. Responsibilities This individual will be responsible for leading the development of content for the Healthcare Business Solutions department, learning programs, and products as part of a 3-year grant. This position will actively collaborate with a cross-functional project teams as well as engage with subject matter experts, organizations, and developers from around the world. The Product Manager will provide direction for the design and development, revision, fiscal management, and quality standards for assigned content and products by (1) working with a project manager to develop project plans, proposals, and procedures; (2) leading the development and production from start to finish; (3) collaborating with American Heart Association staff, volunteers and paid resources; (4) maintaining fiscal oversight and adherence to established budgets; and (5) ongoing evaluation and revisions for the life of the product. Collaborates with the Project Manager, Program Manager, and Marketing to define project scope, establish product requirements, draft contracts, and design the development plan Develops an understanding of the intended audience, science, instructional strategy, and modes of delivery for assigned products. Learns about current user needs and product issues. Demonstrates an understanding of the reasoning behind key policies, practices, and procedures and applies such to content as appropriate Leads project teams in the evaluation, curation, and/or creation of content and product assets that are relevant, valid, and accurate to an industry or topic for a given portfolio of products. Inclusive of the development of multimedia deliverables and assets (e.g., videos, e-learning modules, simulation scenarios, books/e-books, apps, websites, etc.) for assigned programs and products Ensures content and deliverables meet the identified learning objectives, course description, and instructional strategy, and does not compromise the fidelity of the core learning. Ensures content does not compromise the integrity, intent, or the fidelity of the Association Guidelines for CPR and ECC, ASA Guidelines, ACC/American Heart Association Guidelines, or the core science (applicable science) Leads validation of products through beta and pilot tests, coordinates reviews and the collection of feedback, facilitates determination and implementation of changes, and confirms accurate implementation of edits or changes. Manages testing of prototypes throughout development Coordinates and collaborates with Science & Medicine Advisors, Subject Matter Experts, foreign scientific authority, and the project team to achieve approval and signoff of content and product Reviews and triages issues and potential errors or recommendations for the content or product received from the field. Works with technical staff to troubleshoot problems reported by users Ensures project team adherence to a general product development process and established timelines used across the department and develops sub-processes for project-specific tasks as needed Proactively collaborates across project teams to ensure products efficacy and consistently forms productive and collaborative partnerships with team members and stakeholders at all levels Effectively uses informal authority to direct, influence, and motivate others. Solicits and acts on feedback to improve relationships and mission impact Liaises with and fosters relationships with applicable and related science committees and external agencies / organizations Qualifications Bachelor’s degree or equivalent work experience Five (5) years of relevant experience Validated skills and direct participation in the development of scientific, technical, or medical content, including experience working with subject matter experts and a formal review process Experience leading program/product development projects Experience developing eLearning and other digital products that meet accessibility requirements. Validated ability to deliver complex eLearning modules on schedule Project management experience Instructional design experience Healthcare background preferred; required for candidates who will work with advanced healthcare content High level of detail orientation, without losing sight of the broader vision Ability to make a timely decision while considering multiple factors and opinions Ability to travel up to 20% local and overnight stay Compensation & Benefits The expected pay range will be $74,600.00 - $99,500.00. Pay is commensurate with experience; geographic differentials may apply to the pay range. ,The American Heart Association reserves the right to pay more or less than the posted range. The American Heart Association invests in its people. Here are the main components of our total rewards package. Visit Rewards & Benefits to see more details. Compensation – Our goal is to ensure you have a competitive base salary. That’s why we regularly review the market value of jobs and make adjustments, as needed. Performance and Recognition – You are rewarded for achieving success through annual salary planning and incentive programs; eligibility for an incentive program is based on the type of position. Benefits – We offer a wide array of benefits including medical, dental, vision, disability, and life insurance, along with a robust retirement program that includes an employer match and automatic contribution. As a mark of our commitment to employee well-being, we also offer an employee assistance program, employee wellness program and telemedicine, and medical consultation. Professional Development – You can join one of our many Employee Resource Groups (ERG) or be a mentor/mentee in our professional mentoring program. Heart U is the Association’s national online university, with more than 100,000 resources designed to meet your needs and busy schedule. Work-Life Harmonization – The Association offers Paid Time Off (PTO) at a minimum of 16 days per year for new employees. The number of days will increase based on seniority level. You will also have a total of 12 paid holidays off each year, which includes several days off at the end of the year. Tuition Assistance - We support the career development of all employees. This program provides financial assistance to employees who wish to further their education and career in relation to their current duties and responsibilities, or for potential future positions in the organization. The greatest discoveries in health must reach everyone where they are. title: Project Manager (Ontologist)
description: Overview: NV5 Geospatial is actively recruiting a Project Manager (Ontologist) . Strong capabilities in delivering project deliverables in the DoD ontology are required, including ontology development, knowledge graph integration, and supporting DoD geospatial and all-source analysis. The Project Manager (Ontologist) will be based at US Army Garrison Miami/US Southern Command. US citizenship, along with the ability to successfully pass a basic background check for access to US military bases, is required for employment. The successful candidate will have an active TS/SCI clearance. Work Setting: Onsite - US Army Garrison – Miami / SOUTHCOM HQ Potential travel up to 5-15% of the time NV5 is a global technology solutions and consulting services company with a workforce of over 4,500 professionals in more than 100 offices worldwide. NV5’s continued growth has been spurred through strategic investments in firms with unique capabilities to help current and future customers solve the world’s toughest problems. The NV5 family brings together talent across a wide range of markets and fields, including Professional Engineers, Professional Land Surveyors, Architects, Photogrammetrists, GIS Professionals, Software Developers, IT, Project Management Professionals, and more. At NV5 Geospatial, we are a collaboration of intelligent, innovative thinkers who care for each other, our communities, and the environment. We value both heart and head, the diversity of our people, and their experiences because that is how we continue to grow as a leader in our industry and expand our individual and collective potential. Responsibilities: Oversee task execution, ensuring that all ontology development, machine learning governance, and metadata management initiatives align with mission requirements. Manage project timelines, resource allocation, and deliverable tracking, ensuring compliance with spring objectives and stakeholder expectations. Facilitate interagency coordination, ensuring that knowledge representation efforts align with DoD data-sharing agreements and governance frameworks. Qualifications: Minimum Requirements: Master’s degree in Systems Engineering, Project Management, Computer Science, Information Technology, Business Administration, Engineering Management, or a related field, or eleven (11) years of equivalent experience managing complex technical projects particularly in ontology development. Strong background in SysEngOps, DevSecOps, version control systems, and CI/CD pipelines. Demonstrated experience supporting ontology development, knowledge graph integration, or supporting DoD geospatial and all source analysis. Skilled in Python, R, SQL/NoSQL, PostgreSQL, Esri ArcGIS/ArcPy. Demonstrated experience with the Stardog Knowledge Graph platform. Strong analytical skills, attention to detail, and the ability to work collaboratively. Ability to manage and prioritize complex project tasks. Preferred: Project Management Professional (PMP), Certified Scrum Master (CSM), Certified Systems Engineering Professional (CSEP). Portuguese or Spanish language skills. Experience with government IT programs and environments. Clearance Requirement: Active TS/SCI Please be aware that some of our positions may require the ability to obtain a security clearance. Our platform empowers millions of buyers and sellers in more than 190 markets around the world. We’re committed to pushing boundaries and leaving our mark as we reinvent the future of ecommerce for enthusiasts. Our customers are our compass, authenticity thrives, bold ideas are welcome, and everyone can bring their unique selves to work — every day. We're in this together, sustaining the future of our customers, our company, and our planet. Join a team of passionate thinkers, innovators, and dreamers — and help us connect people and build communities to create economic opportunity for all. About the team and the role: Join eBay as a Staff Product Designer - Regulatory, and take on a meaningful role in crafting the future of our high-visibility, fast-growing product. This position presents an outstanding opportunity to influence eBay Live’s global expansion, integrating complex regulatory and compliance requirements into seamless experiences for millions of buyers and sellers. Based in Austin, you will collaborate closely with product, legal, policy, and engineering to deliver world-class, scalable solutions that enhance trust and safety across markets. Lead collaborative workshops and build sprints to align collaborators, explore tradeoffs, and advance complex regulatory and experience challenges. What you will bring: Proven experience in crafting and delivering end-to-end product experiences in close collaboration with product management and engineering. Experience in regulated, trust- and safety-centered, or policy-limited areas, transforming legal and compliance requirements into clear, usable customer experiences. Strong cross-functional collaboration skills with experience working alongside legal, policy, research, and content teams. Ability to operate with ownership in ambiguous problem spaces, framing problems, exploring solutions, and driving work forward with minimal direction. Experience incorporating research, data, and content strategy into build decisions to improve clarity, accessibility, and usability at scale. A method centered on developing across journeys, platforms, and markets, including experiences tailored for both buyers and sellers.
